bead.title: test-scrub: build the e2e/probe CLI binary once via TestMain+sync.Once
bead.description:
PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE: cmd/e2e_test.go:25,183,239 (three tests) + cmd/e2e_smoke_test.go:29 each run `go build` of the full CLI; internal/agent/workerprobe/probe_integration_test.go:44 builds again. Each full build is several seconds, repeated.

PROPOSED FIX: In cmd, add a package-level sync.Once-guarded builder (or TestMain) that builds the ddx binary once to a temp path and exposes it; replace the per-test `go build` calls with the shared path. Same for workerprobe.

NON-SCOPE: same binary, just built once. Behavior/coverage preserved.
bead.acceptance:
1. `go build` of the CLI is invoked at most once per package run (assert via a build-count counter behind the Once, or by elapsed: the 2nd/3rd e2e tests each <2s).
2. TestE2E_BasicWorkflow, TestE2E_TemplateWithVariables, TestE2E_UpdateWorkflow, TestE2ESmokeJourney still pass against the shared binary — coverage preserved.
3. cd cli && go test -run 'TestE2E' ./cmd/ green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
